title: Z3 Fixedpoint Verification Tool Chain
summary: A chain of verification tools built on Z3's Python API. Starting with core SMT constraint solving (N-Queens, graph coloring, scheduling), through the μZ fixedpoint/Datalog engine for program analysis (reachability, Horn clause safety, reaching definitions), to the Spacer property-directed reachability engine for safety verification of transition systems (counter systems, Lamport's Bakery mutual exclusion), an imperative-to-Horn clause translator for program verification, and a CEGAR-style counterexample minimizer with predicate refinement. Each module builds incrementally on Z3's capabilities, forming a complete formal verification pipeline.
instructionPreview: Implement a chain of verification tools using Z3's Python API. The workspace contains a base framework with graph data structures (Graph, Petersen graph, Diamond DAG, Cyclic graph), transition system representations, and Horn clause benchmark files. Implement 5 modules in order:
instruction:
Implement a chain of verification tools using Z3's Python API. The workspace
contains a base framework with graph data structures (Graph, Petersen graph,
Diamond DAG, Cyclic graph), transition system representations, and Horn clause
benchmark files. Implement 5 modules in order:

1. smt_solver/ - SMT constraint solving (N-Queens enumeration, graph coloring,
   job scheduling optimization)
2. fixedpoint/ - Fixedpoint/Datalog program analysis (graph reachability,
   Horn clause safety, reaching definitions)
3. spacer_verifier/ - PDR safety verification (transition system verification,
   Bakery mutual exclusion, counterexample detection)
4. verification/horn_translator.py - Imperative-to-Horn clause translator
   (recursive-descent parser, AST classes, Horn encoding, program verification)
5. verification/cex_minimizer.py - CEGAR counterexample minimization
   (counterexample validation, Craig interpolation, predicate refinement,
   trace minimization, abstraction refinement loop)

Each module has experiments producing KEY: VALUE output lines. Create run.sh
to execute all experiments. See requirements/ for detailed specifications.

moduleDag.description: A chain of verification tools built on Z3's Python API. Starting with core SMT constraint solving (N-Queens, graph coloring, scheduling), through the μZ fixedpoint/Datalog engine for program analysis (reachability, Horn clause safety, reaching definitions), to the Spacer property-directed reachability engine for safety verification of transition systems (counter systems, Lamport's Bakery mutual exclusion), an imperative-to-Horn clause translator for program verification, and a CEGAR-style counterexample minimizer with predicate refinement. Each module builds incrementally on Z3's capabilities, forming a complete formal verification pipeline.

nodes.label: Z3: An Efficient SMT Solver
nodes.description: SMT constraint solving toolkit. Implements N-Queens solution enumeration, graph chromatic number computation, and job-shop scheduling optimization using Z3's Solver and Optimize APIs.

nodes.label: μZ: An Efficient Engine for Fixed-Points with Constraints
nodes.description: Fixedpoint/Datalog program analysis engine. Uses Z3's Fixedpoint API for graph transitive closure, Horn clause safety checking, and reaching definitions analysis via Datalog rules.

nodes.label: Spacer: Property Directed Reachability for Z3
nodes.description: PDR/Spacer-based safety verification. Encodes transition systems as Horn clauses and uses the Spacer engine for property-directed reachability, invariant extraction, and counterexample generation.

nodes.label: Imperative-to-Horn Clause Translator
nodes.description: Translates simple imperative programs (with assignments, loops, branches, assertions) into constrained Horn clauses via recursive- descent parsing and AST-to-Horn encoding for fixedpoint verification.

nodes.label: CEGAR-Style Counterexample Minimization
nodes.description: Counterexample-Guided Abstraction Refinement (CEGAR) loop with concrete counterexample validation, Craig interpolation for predicate discovery, trace minimization, and predicate abstraction management.

edges.label: μZ builds a fixed-point engine on top of Z3's SMT core
Spacer implements property-directed reachability within the μZ framework
Horn translator uses fixedpoint engine for clause verification
CEGAR uses Spacer for counterexample generation and verification
CEGAR refines Horn clause abstractions produced by the translator
